questions.question: A voltmeter has a resistance of $G\text{ ohm}$ and range of $V\text{ volt}$. The resistance required in series to convert it into a voltmeter of range $nV\text{ volt}$ is

Cross-sectional area of a Copper wire is equal to area of a square of length 2 mm. If this copper wire draws 8 A electric current, then find the drift velocity of free electron. Number density of electron in Copper wire is $8 \times 10^{28} \text{ m}^{-3}$.
The internal resistance of a cell of emf 4 V is 0.1 $\Omega$. It is connected to a resistance of 3.9 $\Omega$. The voltage across the cell will be

The ratio of cross-sectional areas of two conducting wires made up of same material and having same length is 1 : 2. What will be the ratio of heat produced per second in the wires, when same current is flowing in them ?

Two electric bulbs are connected one by one across potential difference V. At that time power consumed in them are $P_1$ and $P_2$ respectively. Now, if potential difference V is applied across series combination of these bulbs, what will be total power consumed ?
First order derivation of thermo emf produced in thermo-couple with respect to temperature gives ......

In a Hydrogen atom, the electron moves around the nucleus in a circular orbit of radius $5 \times 10^{-11} \text{ m}$. Its time period is $1.5 \times 10^{-16} \text{ seconds}$. The current associated with the electron motion is ............. (Charge of electron is $1.6 \times 10^{-19} \text{ C}$)
The total current supplied to the given circuit by the battery is ............ [Figure]
A uniform wire has resistance $24 \Omega$. It is bent in the form of a circle. The effective resistance between the two end points on any diameter of the circle is ............

Two bulbs of $250 \text{ V}$ and $100 \text{ W}$ are first connected in series and then in parallel with a supply of $250 \text{ V}$. Total power in each of the cases will be .............. respectively.
The resistance of ideal Voltmeter is .......

options.B: Greater than zero but finite value.

questions.question: The emf of a thermocouple, cold junction of which is kept at $-300^{\circ}\text{C}$ is given by $E = 40\text{ }t + \frac{1}{10}t^2$. The temperature of inversion of thermocouple will be ......
The maximum power dissipated in an external resistance R, when connected to a cell of emf E and internal resistance r, will be ......
The masses of the three wires of Copper are in the ratio $5 : 3 : 1$ and their lengths are in the ratio $1 : 3 : 5$. The ratio of their electrical resistances is ......
The equivalent resistance between A and B in the given circuit is ...... [Diagram]

A wire is bent in the form of circle of radius $2 \text{ m}$. Resistance per unit length of wire is $1/\pi \ \Omega/\text{m}$. Battery of $6 \text{ V}$ is connected between A & B. $\angle AOB = 90^\circ$. Find the current through the battery. [Diagram]
The carbon resistor has three orange bands. The maximum value of resistance offered by the resistor will be ________.

Two wires of same material having lengths and radii in the ratio of $3:4$ and $3:2$ respectively are connected in parallel with a potential source of $6 \text{ V}$. The ratio of currents flowing through them, $I_1:I_2 = $ ________.

A and B are two points on a uniform ring of radius $r$. The resistance of the ring is $R$. $\angle AOB = \theta$ as shown in the figure. The equivalent resistance between points A & B is ________. [Diagram]
Two wires of equal length and equal diameter and having resistivities $\rho_1$ and $\rho_2$ are connected in series. The equivalent resistivity of the combination is ________.
Match the following two columns. [Table]
A voltmeter of a very high resistance is joined in the circuit as shown in figure. The voltage shown by this voltmeter will be ________. [Diagram]
In above circuit if current through 10 $\Omega$ resistor is 2.5 A, value of $R$ is ______. [Diagram]
Brown, Red and Orange coloured bands on a Carbon resistor are followed by silver band. The value of resistor is __________
What is the current in the 4 $\Omega$ resistor when switch $S_1$ is open and switch $S_2$ is closed in the given circuit? [Diagram]
When the temperature of a conductor increases the ratio of conductivity and resistivity ___

You are given $10$ resistors each of resistance $2\Omega$. First they are connected to obtain possible minimum resistance. Then they are connected to obtain possible maximum resistance. The ratio of maximum and minimum resistance is ___
The dimensional formula of mobility is ___
The heat produced per unit time, on passing electric current through a conductor at a given temperature, is directly proportional to the ..............

options.C: Reciprocal of square of electric current

questions.question: A carbon resistor has three bands as brown, black and green in order, What will be the range of resistance it offers?
In the network shown in the figure [Diagram] the equivalent resistance between points X & Y will be ............... $\Omega$. Value of each resistance is $2\Omega$.
Shunt wire should be...........
The emf of a car battery is $12\text{ V}$. If internal resistance of battery is $0.4\text{ }\Omega$, then maximum power drawn from battery is ....... $\text{W}$.

The resistance of the platinum wire of a platinum resistance thermometer at an ice point is $5\text{ }\Omega$ & at steam point is $5.23\text{ }\Omega$. When the thermometer is inserted in a hot bath, the resistance of a platinum wire is $5.795\text{ }\Omega$. Calculate the temperature of the bath.

One electric cell (having emf of $2\text{V}$ & internal resistance of $0.1\text{ }\Omega$) and other electric cell (having emf of $4\text{V}$ & internal resistance of $0.2\text{ }\Omega$) are connected in parallel to each other. Then its equivalent emf will be ........... $\text{V}$
For the which value of Resistance $R = \text{______}$ when galvanometer shows zero deflection for following below electrical circuit. [Diagram]

As following figure $2\text{A}$ current passing through a conducting wire, radius of cross-sectional of wire at point A is $3r$ and point B is $r$ respectively. Then find the ratio of drift velocity at point A & B. [Diagram]

In a potentiometer arrangement, a cell of emf $1.5\text{ V}$ gives a Balance point at $150\text{ cm}$ length of the wire. If the cell is replaced by another cell and the balance point shift to $210\text{ cm}$, what is the emf of the second cell?
A light bulb is rated at $200\text{ W}$ for a $220\text{ V}$ supply. Find the resistance of the bulb

At room temperature ($27^\circ\text{C}$) the resistance of a heating element is $100\text{ }\Omega$. What is the temperature of the element if the resistance is found to be $137\text{ }\Omega$, given that the temperature coefficient of the material of the resistor is $1.35 \times 10^{-4}\text{ }^\circ\text{C}^{-1}$.
For the given following circuit diagram [Figure], the dissipated of electrical power $150\text{ W}$, then find value of Resistance $R = $ ________.

The number density of free electrons in a copper conductor estimated $8.5 \times 10^{28}\text{ m}^{-3}$. How long does an electron take to drift from one end of a wire $6\text{ m}$ long to its other end? The area of cross-section of the wire is $1.0 \times 10^{-6}\text{ m}^2$ and it is carrying a current of $1.5\text{A}$.
Unit of mobility in terms of fundamental units is ________.
A metal rod of length 10 cm and a rectangular cross-section of $1 \text{cm} \times \frac{1}{2} \text{cm}$ is connected to a battery across opposite faces. The resistance will be ________.

options.A: Maximum when the battery is connected across $10 \text{cm} \times 1 \text{cm}$
options.B: Maximum when battery is connected across $1 \text{cm} \times \frac{1}{2} \text{cm}$ faces
options.C: Maximum when the battery is connected across $10 \text{cm} \times \frac{1}{2} \text{cm}$

questions.question: Figure shows 2.0 V potentiometer used for the determination of internal resistance of 1.5 V cell. The balance point of cell in open circuit is 77.4 cm. When a resistor of $9.6 \, \Omega$ is used in the external circuit of the cell, the balanced point shifts to 64.5 cm length of the potentiometer wire. The internal resistance of the cell is ________ $\Omega$. [Figure]
The SI units of the current density is ________.
The magnitude of the drift velocity per unit electric field is known as ________.
As shown in the circuit diagram [Figure] find the value of I ________.

A silver wire has a resistance of $2.1\Omega$ at $27.5^\circ\text{C}$ and a resistance of $2.7\Omega$ at $100^\circ\text{C}$. Then the temperature coefficient of resistivity of silver will be ________.
An ideal ammeter and an ideal voltmeter has resistance ________ $\Omega$ and ________ $\Omega$ respectively.

The drift velocity of an electron is $v_d$ in a conductor of area of cross-section A and carries a current I. Now, the area of cross-section and current flowing through the conductor are double, then new drift velocity of the electron is ________.
As shown in the figure, balanced condition of Wheatstone Bridge is $n = $ ________. [Figure]
In the given circuit, potential difference between points P and Q is ________. [Figure]
